req.title: System-Admin updates expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a expense-claim through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated system-admin can update an expense-claim via REST API, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and correct HTTP status codes. I designed: (1) a positive EP test covering a fully valid update by an admin; (2) a NEG test covering an unauthenticated request (401); (3) a NEG/DT test covering missing mandatory fields (400); (4) a BVA test covering a description field at its maximum allowed length boundary; (5) a STC test covering the state transition of an expense-claim from 'draft' to 'submitted'. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders.

tests.name: test_admin_update_expense_claim_valid
actions: Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
expected: Response body contains the expense-claim entity id
Audit record is written for the update action
trace: R-WEB-042: authenticated admin can update expense-claim
R-WEB-042: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-042: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_update_expense_claim_valid(base_url, admin_token, seed_expense_claim):
    claim_id = seed_expense_claim["id"]
    payload = {
        "title": "Updated Business Trip",
        "amount": 250.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "status": "draft",
        "description": "Hotel and meals"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    audit = <fetch_audit_record>(claim_id, action="update")
    assert audit is not None

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_update_expense_claim_returns_401
actions: Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with no Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_update_expense_claim_returns_401(base_url, seed_expense_claim):
    claim_id = seed_expense_claim["id"]
    payload = {
        "title": "Unauthorized Update",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "status": "draft"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    audit = <fetch_audit_record>(claim_id, action="update")
    assert audit is None

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
expected: Response body contains validation error details
trace: R-WEB-042: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(
    base_url, admin_token, seed_expense_claim
):
    claim_id = seed_expense_claim["id"]
    payload = {"title": "Missing Fields Update"}
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert "errors" in body or "message" in body
    audit = <fetch_audit_record>(claim_id, action="update")
    assert audit is None

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_description_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Max allowed description length is 500 characters
actions: Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with description exactly 500 characters long
trace: R-WEB-042: mandatory field validation at boundary value
R-WEB-042: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-042: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MAX_DESC_LEN = 500

def test_update_expense_claim_description_at_max_length_boundary(
    base_url, admin_token, seed_expense_claim
):
    claim_id = seed_expense_claim["id"]
    description = "A" * MAX_DESC_LEN
    assert len(description) == MAX_DESC_LEN
    payload = {
        "title": "BVA Max Description",
        "amount": 99.99,
        "currency": "USD",
        "status": "draft",
        "description": description
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    audit = <fetch_audit_record>(claim_id, action="update")
    assert audit is not None

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
actions: Create a seed expense-claim with status 'draft' via <create_expense_claim>
Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with status set to 'submitted'
expected: Audit record is written reflecting the state transition
trace: R-WEB-042: audit record written on success
R-WEB-042: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_expense_claim_state_transition_draft_to_submitted(
    base_url, admin_token, seed_draft_expense_claim
):
    claim_id = seed_draft_expense_claim["id"]
    assert seed_draft_expense_claim["status"] == "draft"
    payload = {
        "title": seed_draft_expense_claim["title"],
        "amount": seed_draft_expense_claim["amount"],
        "currency": seed_draft_expense_claim["currency"],
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "submitted"
    audit = <fetch_audit_record>(claim_id, action="update")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit.get("new_status") == "submitted"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_update_expense_claim_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'viewer' not 'admin'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_update_expense_claim_returns_403(
    base_url, viewer_token, seed_expense_claim
):
    claim_id = seed_expense_claim["id"]
    payload = {
        "title": "Forbidden Role Update",
        "amount": 50.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "status": "draft"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {viewer_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    audit = <fetch_audit_record>(claim_id, action="update")
    assert audit is None
